name: Web UI surface > Node > Processes > Agents should have a stop button next to all

failed_actions.message: The web route directly orchestrates chat lookup, process termination, and Goal cancellation instead of delegating this product behavior to a shared capability, making the surface non-thin.
failed_actions.rule: Respect the Foundation-Capabilities-Surfaces boundary.

failed_actions.message: Stop-agent-and-cancel-Goal semantics exist only in the web server route; the shared process service still performs process-only signaling, so there is no authoritative implementation of the combined operation.
failed_actions.rule: Compose existing capabilities with one authoritative implementation per concern.

failed_actions.message: The stop path uses FileProcessSupervisor::signal, which removes the process record immediately after requesting termination, then marks the Goal cancelled without confirming the process exited. A termination-resistant agent can remain alive but untracked under a cancelled Goal.
failed_actions.rule: Keep long-running work supervised with explicit status, cancellation, failure feedback, and recovery.

failed_actions.message: The tests cover a cooperative sleep process only and assert Goal cancellation before checking eventual exit; there is no failure-path evidence for a process that ignores or delays termination, despite changing process cancellation and recovery semantics.
failed_actions.rule: Require evidence proportional to architectural and operational risk.

failed_actions.message: The browser/API path now cancels a linked Goal, while CLI system ps --stop still invokes the shared signal-only service and does not cancel the Goal or handle synthetic chat-agent processes, producing inconsistent process-stop semantics across surfaces.
failed_actions.rule: Preserve one capability across UI, CLI, API, and MCP.

verdict.message: Agent stopping is surface-specific and can cancel a Goal before confirming process exit.

rounds.implementation_report: Added a Stop button to every active Agents row and unified it on POST /api/processes/:id/stop; managed, interactive, and chat agent stops now terminate or close the selected agent, and Goal-linked stops persist and return the Goal as cancelled. Updated process intent and added UI/server regression coverage. Verification: cargo test --lib: 455 passed, 0 failed; node --test tests/*.test.js: 36 passed, 0 failed; cargo check --all-targets: passed; cargo fmt -- --check: passed; git diff --check: passed.
rounds.prompt: Web UI surface > Node > Processes > Agents should have a stop button next to all the agents to kill the process and move the Goal into the cancelled state.

failed_actions.message: If the post-exit ownership check fails, the process has already exited and recover() has removed its registry and identity evidence, but the returned error incorrectly says termination was not requested and omits the confirmed-exit result. The partial outcome is therefore reported inaccurately without usable recovery evidence.
failed_actions.rule: Keep the daemon and surfaces responsive by running agent, Git, quality, lifecycle, and other long-running work through the supervised process substrate with explicit ownership, status, logs, cancellation, failure feedback, and recovery.

failed_actions.message: The final claim and round validation and cancel_goal_summary are separate, unlocked operations. A new round and claim can acquire the Goal after validation but before the unconditional cancellation write, allowing a stop from the previous execution to cancel newer work.
failed_actions.rule: Coordinate parallel or distributed work explicitly through Node ownership, workflow claims and capacity, dependency or Feature ordering, and isolated Git branches or worktrees so concurrent changes remain reviewable and recoverable.

failed_actions.message: The tests install stale claim or round state before stopping; none exercise ownership changing after confirmed process exit or between final validation and Goal cancellation, leaving the concurrency and partial-failure path unverified.
failed_actions.rule: Require evidence proportional to actual architectural and operational risk. Verify the affected behavior with the smallest focused checks that provide confidence, including integration or failure-path evidence when a change alters concurrency, persistence, process, Git, release, recovery, or cross-surface semantics. Update only affected docs/intent pages when documented product intent or an architectural contract changes; do not require unrelated documentation or broad cleanup.

rounds.implementation_report: Committed reviewable recovery c9a92ee5 on top of preserved Round 2 candidate ea2103e3. PID identity is now created only at a process's first registration; legacy or recovered records missing registration-time identity fail closed without signaling, synthesizing evidence, or cancelling a Goal. Workflow agent metadata now records claim_id with execution_id and round_idx, and the shared process-control capability validates the exact claim, execution, current Goal round, and absence of competing active claims both before termination and before Goal cancellation. Added focused success, missing-identity/PID-reuse, stale-execution concurrency, stale-round, mismatch, and resistant-process coverage plus the affected process intent update. Verification: cargo test --lib process_control: 8 passed, 0 failed; cargo test --lib subprocess: 10 passed, 0 failed; cargo test --lib agent_sessions: 3 passed, 0 failed; focused web API stop: 1 passed, 0 failed; focused CLI stop: 1 passed, 0 failed; workflow metadata: 1 passed, 0 failed; focused browser process UI: 4 passed, 0 failed; final cargo test --lib: 463 passed, 0 failed; node --test tests/*.test.js: 37 passed, 0 failed; cargo check --all-targets: passed; cargo fmt --all -- --check: passed; git diff --check, git diff --cached --check, and git show --check: passed. Two earlier full-library attempts exposed a transient interactive-terminal status failure at 462 passed, 1 failed; its isolated rerun passed 1/1 and the final unchanged-source full rerun passed 463/463.
rounds.prompt: Recover rejected candidate a1f6c2e6 from current main while preserving that candidate, its worktree, and all prior provider and Governance evidence. Fix only the five recorded Governance violations. Move the combined stop-agent-and-cancel-linked-Goal behavior into one authoritative shared product capability used consistently by browser, API, CLI system process stop, and MCP surfaces, leaving each surface thin and supporting managed, interactive, chat, and synthetic agent records. Do not remove supervision evidence or mark a linked Goal cancelled merely after requesting termination: retain the process record, confirm the exact owned process has exited, then persist cancellation; if termination is delayed, resisted, mismatched, or fails, keep the Goal non-cancelled and return visible causal failure and recovery evidence. Add focused shared-service and cross-surface coverage, including a termination-resistant or delayed process, identity mismatch, successful confirmed exit before Goal cancellation, and consistent CLI/API/browser behavior. Preserve authentication, cancellation, provider, worktree, Governance, Quality, and human Review boundaries. Do not force merge, rewrite history, discard worktrees, hide failures, or edit durable Goal state directly. Re-run focused process tests, the full library and web suites, all-target check, formatting, and diff checks, and report exact results.

failed_actions.message: Cancellation remains split between two incompatible implementations: WorkflowEngine::cancel signals the process and marks the claim cancelled, while FileProcessControlService confirms exit and cancels the Goal without settling the claim.
failed_actions.rule: Compose the existing Process, Agents, Workflow, Git, state, quality, governance, and evidence capabilities before creating new machinery. Each concern should have one authoritative implementation and source of truth unless the Goal explicitly proposes an architectural replacement.

failed_actions.message: After confirming exit, terminate_owned_and_confirm_exit calls recover before constructing the termination outcome. A cleanup failure therefore returns as an ordinary stop error without confirmed-exit details or a partial-outcome receipt; registry deletion may already have made the process undiscoverable before identity cleanup fails.
failed_actions.rule: Keep the daemon and surfaces responsive by running agent, Git, quality, lifecycle, and other long-running work through the supervised process substrate with explicit ownership, status, logs, cancellation, failure feedback, and recovery.

failed_actions.message: The supposedly atomic settlement only validates the workflow claim; it never marks that claim cancelled. A successful stop can leave the claim running until the killed workflow worker records failure, producing inconsistent Goal and claim state and temporarily retaining capacity ownership.
failed_actions.rule: Coordinate parallel or distributed work explicitly through Node ownership, workflow claims and capacity, dependency or Feature ordering, and isolated Git branches or worktrees so concurrent changes remain reviewable and recoverable.

failed_actions.message: The focused tests use synthetic processes and do not execute a real workflow worker through stop, assert final claim state, exercise the worker-failure race, or inject registry/identity cleanup failure. The atomic-settlement test leaves its claim running and checks only that its execution ID was unchanged.
failed_actions.rule: Require evidence proportional to actual architectural and operational risk. Verify the affected behavior with the smallest focused checks that provide confidence, including integration or failure-path evidence when a change alters concurrency, persistence, process, Git, release, recovery, or cross-surface semantics. Update only affected docs/intent pages when documented product intent or an architectural contract changes; do not require unrelated documentation or broad cleanup.

rounds.implementation_report: Recovered preserved ea2103e3/c9a92ee5 onto current main and committed Round 3 as 2d0d226b. The shared process-stop capability now captures an exact Goal revision/status/round fence, holds cross-process workflow-state and Goal-mutation locks through final claim/execution/competing-owner validation and cancellation, prevents terminal Goal execution retries, reports confirmed process-exit cleanup truthfully, and durably retains post-exit partial-failure receipts with Goal-not-cancelled, cause, and supported recovery evidence. Added deterministic coverage for ownership changing after confirmed exit and for concurrent Round/retry attempts precisely between final validation and cancellation settlement; updated affected process intent. Verification: cargo test --lib process_control: 10 passed, 0 failed; subprocess: 10 passed, 0 failed; agent_sessions: 3 passed, 0 failed; work_items: 23 passed, 0 failed; focused web API: 1 passed, 0 failed; CLI: 1 passed, 0 failed; MCP: 1 passed, 0 failed; workflow metadata: 1 passed, 0 failed; focused browser: 4 passed, 0 failed; final cargo test --lib: 465 passed, 0 failed; final node --test tests/*.test.js: 37 passed, 0 failed; cargo check --all-targets passed; cargo fmt --all -- --check passed; git diff --check, git diff --cached --check, and git show --check passed. An earlier full-library run found web_server_retries_workflow_executions returning 404 (464 passed, 1 failed); the target-less retry compatibility guard was corrected, its focused rerun passed, and the unchanged final full rerun passed 465/465.
rounds.prompt: Recover Governance-rejected candidate c9a92ee5 from current main while preserving c9a92ee5, ea2103e3, all earlier candidates, worktrees, rounds, and provider/Governance evidence. Fix only the three newly recorded violations in the shared process-stop capability. After exact process exit is confirmed, retain and report truthful partial-outcome evidence if the post-exit ownership fence fails: do not claim termination was unrequested, and include confirmed-exit, registry/identity cleanup, Goal-not-cancelled, causal ownership mismatch, and supported recovery details. Make the final exact claim, execution, round, Goal revision/status, and absence-of-competing-owner validation atomic with the linked Goal cancellation write, so a newer round or claim cannot appear between validation and cancellation and stale process work can never cancel newer Goal work. Add deterministic production-path concurrency coverage that changes ownership after confirmed process exit and specifically between the last validation and cancellation settlement, plus assertions for accurate retained partial-failure evidence. Re-run focused process-control, subprocess, agent-session, web API, CLI, MCP/browser, workflow metadata, full library and web suites, all-target check, formatting, and diff checks, reporting exact results. Preserve one shared capability across surfaces and all provider, authentication, cancellation, worktree, Governance, Quality, and human Review boundaries. Do not force merge, rewrite history, discard worktrees, hide failures, or edit durable Goal state directly.

failed_actions.message: cancel_workflow_execution treats an empty managed-process lookup as valid ownership and cancels the running claim and linked Goal without confirming process exit. During worker registration races or lost registry evidence, the worker can remain or start running under a cancelled Goal.
failed_actions.rule: Keep the daemon and surfaces responsive by running agent, Git, quality, lifecycle, and other long-running work through the supervised process substrate with explicit ownership, status, logs, cancellation, failure feedback, and recovery.

failed_actions.message: Cancellation settlement is not failure-atomic: it persists the claim as cancelled and releases capacity before writing the Goal cancellation. Capacity-release or Goal-write failure can leave a cancelled claim with an active Goal and either retained or prematurely released capacity.
failed_actions.rule: Coordinate parallel or distributed work explicitly through Node ownership, workflow claims and capacity, dependency or Feature ordering, and isolated Git branches or worktrees so concurrent changes remain reviewable and recoverable.

failed_actions.message: The added tests cover a registered worker, ownership races, and registry/identity cleanup failures, but do not cover target-bound cancellation before worker registration or failures between claim persistence, capacity release, and the Goal write.
failed_actions.rule: Require evidence proportional to actual architectural and operational risk. Verify the affected behavior with the smallest focused checks that provide confidence, including integration or failure-path evidence when a change alters concurrency, persistence, process, Git, release, recovery, or cross-surface semantics. Update only affected docs/intent pages when documented product intent or an architectural contract changes; do not require unrelated documentation or broad cleanup.

governance_rule_actions.rule: Coordinate parallel or distributed work explicitly through Node ownership, workflow claims and capacity, dependency or Feature ordering, and isolated Git branches or worktrees so concurrent changes remain reviewable and recoverable.

governance_rule_actions.message: The added tests cover a registered worker, ownership races, and registry/identity cleanup failures, but do not cover target-bound cancellation before worker registration or failures between claim persistence, capacity release, and the Goal write.
governance_rule_actions.rule: Require evidence proportional to actual architectural and operational risk. Verify the affected behavior with the smallest focused checks that provide confidence, including integration or failure-path evidence when a change alters concurrency, persistence, process, Git, release, recovery, or cross-surface semantics. Update only affected docs/intent pages when documented product intent or an architectural contract changes; do not require unrelated documentation or broad cleanup.

rounds.implementation_report: Committed d81385dc on Round 4 after preserving and replaying the Round 3 stack. WorkflowEngine::cancel now delegates to the authoritative FileProcessControlService; confirmed process exit is durably recorded before registry/identity cleanup; cleanup failures retain accurate partial receipts; exact claim cancellation, Goal cancellation, and capacity release settle under the workflow/Goal fences; terminal worker failure cannot overwrite cancelled claim/Goal state. Added real workflow-worker cancellation/race coverage, registry and identity cleanup fault injection, final claim/capacity assertions, and updated process intent. Verification: process_control 12 passed, 0 failed; real workflow worker 1 passed, 0 failed; subprocess 10 passed, 0 failed; agent_sessions 3 passed, 0 failed; web API 1 passed, 0 failed; CLI 1 passed, 0 failed; MCP 1 passed, 0 failed; workflow metadata 1 passed, 0 failed; browser process UI 4 passed, 0 failed; final cargo test --lib 468 passed, 0 failed; node --test tests/*.test.js 37 passed, 0 failed; cargo check --all-targets passed; cargo fmt --all -- --check passed; git diff --check, git diff --cached --check, and git show --check passed. Earlier full-library attempts each exposed the same pre-existing intra-test quality-settings EOF race in one of two live-replenishment tests (467 passed, 1 failed); both isolated reruns passed, the overly broad timing change was narrowed, and the final unchanged-source default full run passed 468/468.
rounds.prompt: Recover Governance-rejected candidate 2d0d226b from current main while preserving 2d0d226b, c9a92ee5, ea2103e3, all earlier candidates, worktrees, rounds, and provider/Governance evidence. Fix only the four newly recorded violations in the shared process-stop capability. Establish one authoritative cancellation implementation shared by WorkflowEngine::cancel and FileProcessControlService instead of split semantics. After confirmed process exit, construct and retain truthful partial-outcome evidence before cleanup so registry or identity cleanup failure cannot become an ordinary stop error or erase confirmed-exit and recovery details. Atomically settle the exact workflow claim as cancelled together with the fenced Goal cancellation so successful stop cannot leave a running claim or retained capacity, while preserving exact execution, round, Goal revision/status, and competing-owner protections. Add deterministic production-path coverage using a real workflow worker stopped through the shared capability; assert final Goal and claim state, cover the worker-failure race, and inject registry and identity cleanup failures with accurate durable partial-failure receipts. Re-run focused process-control, subprocess, agent-session, web API, CLI, MCP/browser, workflow metadata, full library and web suites, all-target check, formatting, and diff checks, reporting exact results. Preserve one shared capability across surfaces and all provider, authentication, cancellation, worktree, Governance, Quality, and human Review boundaries. Do not force merge, rewrite history, discard worktrees, hide failures, or edit durable Goal state directly.

failed_actions.message: The settlement journal has no replay path. If execution stops after claim persistence or capacity release but before Goal persistence, a retry sees the cancelled claim and returns already_cancelled without consulting the journal, leaving the Goal and capacity inconsistent and unrecoverable through the shared capability.
failed_actions.rule: Keep the daemon and surfaces responsive by running agent, Git, quality, lifecycle, and other long-running work through the supervised process substrate with explicit ownership, status, logs, cancellation, failure feedback, and recovery.

failed_actions.message: Successful settlement uses a WorkflowEngine without target-app context; persisting the cancelled claim therefore replaces the stored workflow policy with defaults, silently discarding configured limits, Node, provider, and target-app identity.
failed_actions.rule: Coordinate parallel or distributed work explicitly through Node ownership, workflow claims and capacity, dependency or Feature ordering, and isolated Git branches or worktrees so concurrent changes remain reviewable and recoverable.

failed_actions.message: The tests cover in-process errors that execute rollback, but do not cover interruption and restart between durable writes or assert preservation of a non-default workflow policy, leaving both persistence defects unverified.
failed_actions.rule: Require evidence proportional to actual architectural and operational risk. Verify the affected behavior with the smallest focused checks that provide confidence, including integration or failure-path evidence when a change alters concurrency, persistence, process, Git, release, recovery, or cross-surface semantics. rounds.implementation_report: Committed Round 5 as 18bac9080a09689263971e7ed648c6f516191e20. Target-bound cancellation now fails closed when no managed-process record exists; a shared spawn-to-registration fence prevents workers from starting under settled claims; exact claim, capacity, and Goal writes are journaled and rolled back together on failure; confirmed-exit receipts retain ownership and support retry recovery. Added real-worker pre-registration coverage, post-cancellation launch rejection, and injected failures after claim persistence, capacity release, and Goal persistence with exact state and recovery assertions; updated process intent and shared-capacity coverage. Focused results: process_control 14/14, real pre-registration worker 1/1, real worker settlement 1/1, workflow replenishment 2/2, subprocess 11/11, agent sessions 3/3, web API 1/1, CLI 1/1, MCP 1/1, workflow metadata 1/1, browser process UI 4/4. Initial full library run: 470 passed, 2 failed (obsolete zero-process cancellation expectation; known quality-settings EOF race); both isolated reruns passed. Two subsequent unchanged-source full library runs passed 472/472, including the final run. Final web suite passed 37/37; cargo check --all-targets, cargo fmt --all -- --check, git diff --check, git diff --cached --check, and git show --check passed. Worktree is clean; all prior candidate commits and worktrees were preserved.
rounds.prompt: Recover Governance-rejected candidate d81385dc from current main while preserving d81385dc, 2d0d226b, c9a92ee5, ea2103e3, all earlier candidates, worktrees, rounds, tests, and provider/Governance evidence. Fix only the three newly recorded violations in the authoritative shared cancellation capability. Fail closed when a target-bound running workflow claim has no managed-process record: an empty lookup during registration or lost-registry races is not proof of process exit and must not cancel the claim or Goal; coordinate registration and cancellation so a worker cannot remain or start under cancelled work. Make linked process exit, exact claim cancellation, Goal cancellation, and capacity settlement failure-atomic across their durable writes, with either one committed terminal outcome or truthful recoverable partial-failure evidence and no silently inconsistent active Goal, claim, or capacity state. Add deterministic production-path tests for cancellation before worker registration and injected failures between claim persistence, capacity release, and Goal persistence, asserting exact final durable states and supported recovery. Re-run the focused service, real workflow-worker, workflow scheduling, subprocess, agent-session, web API, CLI, MCP/browser, workflow metadata, full library and web suites, all-target check, formatting, and diff checks, reporting every initial failure and final result accurately. Preserve one shared capability across surfaces and all provider, authentication, cancellation, worktree, Governance, Quality, and human Review boundaries. Do not force merge, rewrite history, discard worktrees, hide failures, or edit durable Goal state directly.

rounds.implementation_report: Committed reviewable Round 6 as ebc7d269 after replaying the preserved Round 5 stack onto current main. Cancellation journals now durably store exact workflow, capacity, and Goal before/after states; a recreated shared service replays them before any already-cancelled short circuit, finishes only the linked claim/lease/Goal changes, preserves unrelated concurrent claims and capacity, and completes retained process receipts. Cancellation persistence no longer uses target-less default policy: successful and recovered settlement preserve non-default limits, Node, provider, and target identity byte-for-byte. Added deterministic restart tests for interruption after claim persistence, capacity release, and Goal persistence plus successful non-default-policy coverage; updated process intent. Verification: process_control 16/16; real pre-registration worker 1/1; real worker settlement 1/1; workflow replenishment 2/2; subprocess 11/11; agent sessions 3/3; web API 1/1; CLI 1/1; MCP 1/1; workflow metadata 1/1; focused browser processes 4/4; final full library 474/474; final web suite 37/37; cargo check --all-targets, cargo fmt --all -- --check, git diff --check, git diff --cached --check, and git show --check passed. Initial full-library verification exposed an unrelated interactive-terminal cleanup race (473 passed, 1 failed); its isolated rerun passed and an unchanged-source rerun passed 474/474. A later full run exposed a new policy-test-only /proc disappearance race (473 passed, 1 failed); the unnecessary subprocess was removed from that success-only test while real restart tests retained process-exit coverage, focused reruns passed, and the final full run passed 474/474. Worktree is clean; original 18bac908 and all prior worktrees remain preserved.
rounds.prompt: Recover Governance-rejected candidate 18bac908 from current main while preserving 18bac908, d81385dc, 2d0d226b, c9a92ee5, ea2103e3, all earlier candidates, worktrees, rounds, tests, and provider/Governance evidence. Fix only the three newly recorded violations in the authoritative shared cancellation capability. Add durable settlement-journal recovery and replay before any already-cancelled short circuit so interruption or restart after claim persistence or capacity release can deterministically finish or restore the exact linked Goal, claim, and capacity outcome through the shared capability. Preserve the complete existing WorkflowAutomation policy and target-app context when persisting cancellation; never construct or save a default-context engine that can erase configured limits, Node, provider, remote, or target identity. Add deterministic restart-level tests that interrupt after each durable write, recreate the service, replay the journal, and assert exact Goal/claim/capacity recovery; also seed and assert a non-default workflow policy survives successful and recovered cancellation byte-for-byte except for the intended claim transition. Re-run the focused service, real workflow-worker, workflow scheduling, subprocess, agent-session, web API, CLI, MCP/browser, workflow metadata, full library and web suites, all-target check, formatting, and diff checks, reporting every initial failure and final result accurately. Preserve one shared capability across surfaces and all provider, authentication, cancellation, worktree, Governance, Quality, and human Review boundaries. Do not force merge, rewrite history, discard worktrees, hide failures, or edit durable Goal state directly.

diagnostics: No Quality tests or migrated commands are configured; evaluation was a no-op.

failed_actions.message: The terminal surface still implements agent stopping directly through terminal_stop_response and stop_agent_session instead of delegating Goal-linked termination and cancellation to the shared process-control capability.
failed_actions.rule: Respect the Foundation-Capabilities-Surfaces boundary: durable concepts belong in the model and target-app foundation, product behavior and orchestration belong in shared capabilities, and UI, CLI, API, desktop, and agent surfaces stay thin and semantically consistent.

failed_actions.message: Goal Agents have two stop implementations: /api/processes/:id/stop performs confirmed exit and linked Goal/claim/capacity settlement, while /api/terminal/:session/stop only signals or kills the session and leaves that settlement to other paths.
failed_actions.rule: Compose the existing Process, Agents, Workflow, Git, state, quality, governance, and evidence capabilities before creating new machinery. Each concern should have one authoritative implementation and source of truth unless the Goal explicitly proposes an architectural replacement.

failed_actions.message: Cancellation recovery is incomplete: settlement journals use write-and-rename without file or directory synchronization, and a rollback_failed outcome after successful Goal restoration has a new Goal revision that prepare_goal_cancellation_replay rejects as neither journal before-state nor after-state.
failed_actions.rule: Keep the daemon and surfaces responsive by running agent, Git, quality, lifecycle, and other long-running work through the supervised process substrate with explicit ownership, status, logs, cancellation, failure feedback, and recovery.

failed_actions.message: Coverage verifies clean rollback and interruption replay but not rollback failure after Goal restoration, journal durability boundaries, or stopping a workflow Goal Agent through the browser terminal Stop control.
failed_actions.rule: Require evidence proportional to actual architectural and operational risk. Verify the affected behavior with the smallest focused checks that provide confidence, including integration or failure-path evidence when a change alters concurrency, persistence, process, Git, release, recovery, or cross-surface semantics. Update only affected docs/intent pages when documented product intent or an architectural contract changes; do not require unrelated documentation or broad cleanup.

failed_actions.message: The browser Goal-terminal Stop button calls the terminal-specific endpoint, so it can kill the same Goal Agent without the linked cancellation semantics used by the Processes UI, CLI, API process route, and MCP.
failed_actions.rule: Keep Refine agent-first: delegate adaptable reasoning and execution to installed agents through shared context and tools instead of duplicating it in bespoke product logic or surface-specific controls. Add first-class functionality when it provides durable coordination, state, synchronization, supervision, evidence, recovery, accessibility, deterministic integrity, or a materially better shared human and agent surface; preserve one capability across UI, CLI, API, and MCP.

rounds.implementation_report: Recovered Round 6 onto requested main 562abe5f as a reviewable eight-commit stack ending at 6f0f7c63. Preserved authoritative shared cancellation, confirmed-exit receipts, durable exact settlement replay, workflow policy/target identity, CLI/API/MCP/browser parity, current-main active Refine executable and ./r prompt guidance, cached live diagnostics, and all prior candidates/worktrees. Resolved current-main workflow/Goal revision and lock-order conflicts with monotonic workflow/claim/Goal revisions and coordination-before-state/Goal mutation; retained process-group signaling. Refreshed origin/main to 94273722: selectively included the independent 87029e72 benchmark-fixture patch while keeping transcript candidate 9b7d9d33 out as instructed. Initial focused build failed on three new WorkflowClaim fields; fixed. Initial process_control run had 13 pass, 2 fail, 1 hang from revision/lock integration; each was fixed and focused reruns passed. Initial default full library run had 489 pass, 2 fail: a warmed-create fixture failure also reproduced on untouched main, and a transient /proc identity disappearance whose isolated rerun passed. A tmpfs full rerun passed 491/491 before refreshed main; after selectively applying main's benchmark-fixture fix, the final default full library run passed 491/491. Final focused: process_control 16/16, subprocess 11/11, agent_sessions 4/4, real pre-registration worker 1/1, real worker settlement 1/1, workflow replenishment 2/2, web API 1/1, CLI 1/1, MCP 1/1, workflow metadata 1/1, browser processes 4/4, cached diagnostics 1/1, Ready Merge fence 1/1, managed-registry cancellation 1/1. Final web suite 38/38; cargo check --all-targets passed; cargo fmt --all -- --check passed; git diff/check, cached diff/check, and commit whitespace checks passed. Worktree clean; no unresolved three-way conflict markers; 9b7d9d33 is not an ancestor.
rounds.prompt: Recover the preserved Round 6 candidate ebc7d2699abcb6ffe26aaccac826197d2ccdb163 from current main 562abe5fff5c81713300bf7d539f5fc38e61aacb. A read-only three-way preview now shows 26 conflict markers because the candidate is 15 main commits behind, including real conflicts in src/process/agent_sessions.rs, src/process/subprocess/mod.rs, src/surfaces/web_server/support/terminal.rs, src/tools/product/work_items/mod.rs, and src/workflow/mod.rs, with overlapping changes also in CLI/web/process/work-item tests and routes. Preserve ebc7d269, all earlier rounds/candidates/worktrees, provider/Governance/Quality evidence, and the complete Round 6 intent: authoritative shared cancellation, durable settlement replay, exact workflow/Goal/claim/capacity outcomes, policy and target identity preservation, process receipts, and surface parity. Reconstruct or replay that intent on the current main rather than force-merging or deleting conflict evidence. Retain all already-merged main behavior, especially Goal Agent active Refine executable and ./r prompt guidance from 006f6652 and live cached diagnostics process health from fa0a43ff. The separate transcript handoff candidate 9b7d9d33 remains unapproved at review: do not cherry-pick, merge, or assume it; keep this recovery semantically compatible where practical without crossing that review boundary. Add or update focused conflict-sensitive tests, rerun the Round 6 focused suites plus full library/web/all-target/format/diff gates, and report every initial and final failure exactly. Before completion, refresh current main evidence and confirm the resulting candidate has no unresolved three-way conflict markers. Do not approve or merge, force Git, rewrite history, discard any worktree, hide provider/authentication/test failures, or edit durable Goal state directly.

Recover Governance-failed Round 7 candidate 6f0f7c63b0efb399698e75b656430e5bccc6d931 onto the exact current main 94273722bcbfe4d14b669f21403f4615ff6b9ce4. Preserve Round 7 and every earlier candidate, Round, worktree, test result, provider result, and Governance or Quality record. Current main now includes both independently approved fixes 87029e72 and 9b7d9d33; preserve both. A read-only current-main three-way preview of 6f0f7c63 has two conflict markers, with the material conflict in src/process/subprocess/mod.rs: reconcile the approved artifact_handoff_path lease and transcript protection with the Round 7 process identity registry, confirmed-exit receipts, process-group signaling, cancellation journal, replay, revision fences, and cleanup semantics. Preserve the transcript lease through reader join, transcript read, SSE grace, and final cleanup; preserve overlapping agent_sessions and web behavior even where Git auto-merges it. Fix every Round 7 Governance action: make the browser Goal-terminal stop path delegate Goal-linked termination and Goal, claim, and capacity settlement to the same authoritative shared process-control capability as the Processes API, CLI, and MCP; remove or narrow terminal-specific stop orchestration so it cannot bypass confirmed exit and settlement. Make settlement journal persistence actually durable across crash boundaries, including file and parent-directory synchronization around write and rename. Make replay accept and deterministically finish or restore rollback_failed after successful Goal restoration even when restoration creates a new Goal revision. Add focused failure-path tests for terminal Stop on a workflow Goal Agent, durable journal boundaries, and rollback failure after Goal restoration and process recreation. Retain exact workflow policy and target identity, monotonic revisions, coordination-before-state and Goal mutation lock order, cancellation capacity accounting, and surface parity without introducing nested coordination-lock self-deadlocks. Re-run transcript handoff tests, process_control, subprocess, agent_sessions, workflow worker and replenishment, browser and web API, CLI and MCP, workflow metadata, full library and web suites, cargo check --all-targets, cargo fmt --all -- --check, git diff --check, and a current-main merge-tree preview.
